title: baphia dewevrei subsp. marquesii
intro: Baphia dewevrei subsp. marquesii is a plant species found in the tropical forests of East Africa, classified as a subspecies of the Baphia dewevrei plant. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, such as relieving fever, reducing pain and swelling, and treating skin conditions. Baphia dewevrei subsp. marquesii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal use.
